examples.title: Read Command Output with popen()
examples.description: Demonstrates reading output from 'ls' command using popen() in read mode
examples.explanation:
1. popen() executes 'ls -l' and returns FILE pointer
2. fgets() reads pipe output line by line
3. pclose() closes stream and waits for command completion
4. WEXITSTATUS checks child process exit status

examples.title: Write to Command with popen()
examples.description: Sends data to 'grep' command through popen() in write mode
examples.explanation:
1. popen() with 'w' mode connects to grep's stdin
2. fprintf() writes filtered data to command
3. Output shows only lines containing 'important'
4. Always close with pclose() to avoid zombies

examples.title: Error Handling with popen()
examples.description: Proper error checking for popen/pclose operations
examples.explanation:
1. Check popen() return for NULL
2. Use errno to identify errors
3. Check pclose() return value
4. WEXITSTATUS interprets exit code

examples.title: Algorithm: popen() Implementation Steps
examples.description: Manual implementation using pipe(), fork(), and exec()
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PopenEmulation
BEGIN
 1. Create pipe using pipe()
 2. Fork new process
 3. IF child process:
 a. Close unused pipe end
 b. dup2() to redirect stdin/stdout
 c. Close original pipe descriptors
 d. execvp() target command
 4. ELSE parent process:
 a. Close unused pipe end
 b. Return FILE stream for remaining pipe end
END
examples.explanation:
1. pipe() creates communication channel
2. fork() creates child process
3. dup2() connects pipe to stdin/stdout
4. exec() replaces child process image
5. Parent manages pipe as stream
